acceptance.rule: all three fuzzers comparable at CHAIN_DEPTH=1 (shallow chain, terminal literal already needs I2S so cmplog/vpc lead value_profile); cmplog and value_profile both >> behind value_profile_cmplog by CHAIN_DEPTH=4; cmplog≈0 and value_profile≈0 while value_profile_cmplog>0 at CHAIN_DEPTH=8

design_basis: CHAIN_DEPTH scales the UPSTREAM VP-climbable requirement while the terminal I2S literal width (TERM_BYTES) is fixed wide. As depth grows, cmplog (I2S, no VP gradient) cannot navigate the deepening sequential chain and stalls before ever reaching the terminal literal; value_profile (VP gradient, no I2S) climbs the chain but cannot synthesize the wide terminal literal. Only value_profile_cmplog has both, so its advantage over each single-technique loser grows monotonically with the knob — the synergy signature.
notes: CHAIN_DEPTH=1: trivial 1-byte chain; the terminal TERM_BYTES literal dominates, so cmplog and vpc (both I2S) crash readily while value_profile lags — all involved fuzzers still produce crashes, gap small.
CHAIN_DEPTH=2: chain still cheap; cmplog can occasionally brute the 2-byte head, vpc fastest, value_profile slower (no I2S terminal).

CHAIN_DEPTH=4: cmplog's blind reproduction of the 4-byte chain head collapses (~1/256^4) so it rarely reaches the terminal gate; value_profile climbs the chain but still stalls on the terminal literal; vpc clears both — clear stratification vpc >> {cmplog, value_profile}.

CHAIN_DEPTH=8: cmplog ≈ 0 (8-byte blind chain) and value_profile ≈ 0 (terminal literal still unsolved); value_profile_cmplog still > 0 because it climbs the chain via VP gradient and plants the terminal literal via I2S.
